claims.claim_text: In Kubernetes 1.35+, the Horizontal Pod Autoscaler can scale workloads using CPU, memory, and custom metrics via the autoscaling/v2 API.
claims.span_hint: section: Choose a Provisioning Strategy > Reactive Autoscaling
claims.rationale: The v1.35 HPA docs directly state that autoscaling/v2 supports scaling on memory and custom metrics, and the same page documents resource-metric scaling for CPU.

claims.claim_text: Kubernetes scheduling decisions use Pod resource requests to decide placement.
claims.span_hint: section: Control Cost Without Undermining Reliability > Right-Sizing and Resource Requests
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes resource-management docs explicitly say kube-scheduler uses container resource requests to decide which node to place a Pod on.

claims.claim_text: If Pod requests are set too high, nodes can appear full to the scheduler even when real utilization is low.
claims.span_hint: section: Control Cost Without Undermining Reliability > Right-Sizing and Resource Requests
claims.rationale: The same resource-management page directly says the scheduler can refuse placement even when actual memory or CPU usage is low because it schedules against requested capacity.

claims.claim_text: CPU limits can throttle latency-sensitive services, while missing memory limits can let one pod pressure a node.
claims.span_hint: section: Control Cost Without Undermining Reliability > Right-Sizing and Resource Requests
claims.anchor_text: Limits add another layer: CPU limits can throttle latency-sensitive services, while missing memory limits can let one pod pressure a node.
claims.suggested_rewrite: Limits add another layer: CPU limits can throttle workloads, and poor memory sizing can destabilize a node under load.
claims.rationale: The docs directly support CPU throttling, but the second clause is more specific than the allowlisted sources support as written, so the rewrite keeps the lesson while dropping the over-precise mechanism.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Docs: Horizontal Pod Autoscaling
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary reference for HPA metrics, scaling behavior, stabilization windows, and autoscaling trade-offs discussed in the module.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Docs: Resource Management for Pods and Containers
further_reading.why_relevant: Directly explains requests, limits, scheduler placement, throttling, and the resource-accounting semantics behind right-sizing decisions.

further_reading.title: Kubernetes Docs: Node Autoscaling
further_reading.why_relevant: Complements workload autoscaling with the cluster-level provisioning and consolidation behavior needed for end-to-end capacity planning.

notes: Most numeric examples in this module are explicitly pedagogical or hypothetical, so they do not need citation entries. I excluded quiz items, exercise scaffolding, Mermaid diagrams, code blocks, and generic SRE heuristics unless they made a concrete Kubernetes-behavior claim.
